title: The Physics Behind the Poo Emoji’s Shape
gist: A Nature Communications study uses the lugworm — which defecates upward against gravity from a U-shaped burrow — to probe the physics that produces the coiled, spiral morphology of feces across species, a topic that once fascinated Charles Darwin.

insights.claim: Lugworms, living head-down in U-shaped beach burrows, excrete a thick stream of wet sandy excrement upward against gravity, an anomaly used to study the physics of feces in a Nature Communications study.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Soft-matter/biomechanics curiosity; limited direct investable signal.
insights.quote: the lugworm excretes a thick stream of wet, sandy excrement upward, right on to the beach
insights.locator: When nature calls, the lugworm excretes

insights.claim: The characteristic coiled morphologies of animal excreta appear across diverse species, a recurring pattern that fascinated Charles Darwin, who marveled at earthworms' spiral-shaped castings.
insights.speaker: Jake Currie
insights.so_what: Cross-species pattern hints at shared underlying mechanics worth a soft-matter framework.
insights.quote: Anyone who has observed animal excreta has likely wondered about the characteristic coiled morphologies that appear across diverse species
insights.locator: reads the first line of the study
